ground_truth.title: Flight hacks: When to book your flights for the best prices
ground_truth.author: Daniela Sunde-Brown
ground_truth.main_content:
One of the biggest expenses when planning a big trip is often the flights, but there are a handful of flight hacks you can use to save hundreds off the price of a ticket. Naturally, shopping around is one of the top ways to find cheap flights, but there are other tips and tricks to help you find the best rates.

Finding cheap flights is a matter of you against the system. Naturally, every airline wants to earn more money from every customer, but they also don't want to fly half-empty planes. Airlines use dynamic pricing, meaning the seat price fluctuates due to supply and demand.

For domestic trips within the US, booking just one or two months ahead is often the best time for the cheapest flights. For longer journeys from the United States to Europe or beyond you want to start planning much further in advance. Long-haul flights are still best booked six to eight months in advance.

Top flight hacks for 2025: Flexibility is key - keep your travel dates and destinations open. If you can be flexible, then you can find good deals. Consider the day of the week you book on - some people say Tuesday is the cheapest day to book and fly. Look at airline loyalty programs and benefits. Use flight comparison tools effectively with filters and features. Sign up for airline newsletters for last-minute deals and fare sales.

Airlines use dynamic pricing, meaning the seat price fluctuates due to supply and demand.
For domestic trips within the US, booking just one or two months ahead is often the best time for the cheapest flights.
Long-haul flights are still best booked six to eight months in advance.
If you can be flexible, then you can find good deals. This often means traveling off-peak and off-season.
